Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 09:38 ET
Dispersion strategy signals flash as index puts clash with single-name calls
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. The 1.08 divergence between index puts (2.02) and single-name calls (0.94) marks the largest institutional dispersion hedge setup since October.
  • 2. Correlation at 0.03 confirms stock-specific regime while 14 names in backwardation price elevated near-term event risk ahead of Thursday GDP.
  • 3. NVDA's 160K+ call blocks across strikes signal pre-earnings positioning with Millennium's 93% Q4 add providing institutional validation.
NARRATIVE — bullish flow, defensive hedging

The most compelling signal today is the extreme P/C divergence — index puts at 2.02 while single-name ratios sit at 0.94, creating a 1.08 spread that screams institutional dispersion positioning. This setup, combined with implied correlation at just 0.03, suggests hedge funds are betting on stock-specific outcomes while hedging broad market exposure into Thursday's GDP print. The 14 names in backwardation, including ARM, COIN, and JPM, confirm elevated near-term event risk is being priced aggressively. NVDA dominates single-name flow with 160K+ calls across June 140C and 170C strikes, coinciding with Tuesday's earnings and Millennium's 93% Q4 position increase that flipped their stance from puts to conviction calls. The vol surface shows TECH BULL/SKEW FEAR divergence in XLK, indicating systematic demand for tech upside while defensive skew persists — a classic pre-earnings positioning pattern when institutional money wants exposure but retail remains cautious. Positive dealer gamma at $2.58B SPY creates a dampening environment that should contain volatility into the GDP release, but the put-heavy index flow suggests institutions are paying up for protection despite the technical backdrop. With VIX term structure in healthy contango at +2.04, the setup favors dispersion trades over directional bets.

Layer 1: Sentiment +26Layer 2: GEX +$2.58BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.08Layer 4: IC 0.03
